mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 8% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to reduced corporate earnings visibility and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite elevated unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and GDP signals robust supply-side expansion, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate profitability.
failure_condition: Both GDPC1 and INDPRO show growth but SPX 60d returns are negative in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% prompts central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 60% of instances when CPI exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 570k signals strong consumer demand and business investment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in > 50% of cases when housing starts exceed 570k.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in payroll employment during a recession signals weak labor market dynamics, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 50% of instances when payroll employment declines.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates strong demand pressures, which correlate with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Both RSAFS and CPIAUCSL show growth but SPX 20d returns are negative in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production above 95 signals improved supply chain efficiency and corporate capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in > 50% of instances when INDPRO exceeds 95.
